name: Qwen2-57B-A14B
archDisplay: Mixture-of-Experts Transformer decoder (57B total / 14B active per token)
note: Gen-2 MoE (14B active of 57B total) matching ~30B-dense-class quality; Apache 2.0.
tpsNote: 57B total weights exceed a single RTX 4090's 24GB VRAM even at Q4.

results.notes: Base model, 10-shot. A prior audit pass dropped this exact row, claiming the blog's Qwen2-57B-A14B base-performance table 'has no HellaSwag row at all' — that claim is incorrect: I live-fetched qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen2/ and the base table for Qwen2-57B-A14B explicitly lists HellaSwag=85.2 alongside MMLU/BBH/etc. Re-adding with the correct primary source (the blog itself, not the arxiv report).
Instruct variant (Qwen2-57B-A14B-Instruct); same value on the blog
Instruct variant, LiveCodeBench v1; same value on the blog
Instruct variant, strict-prompt subset; only in the technical report (Table 7), not on the blog
Instruct variant, MGSM 0-shot CoT, from the blog's multilingual-capability table

LogicKor official leaderboard, model_id Qwen/Qwen2-57B-A14B-Instruct (exact hf-link match), default prompt strategy [score corrected during adversarial verify: proposed 6.61 -> 6.6, per verifier: Verified via two independent fetches of https://lk.instruct.kr/ (LogicKor leaderboard). The model "Qwen/Qwen2-57B-A14B-Instruct" does appear with prompt strategy "default" (matching the claim's notes), confirming the model/benchmark/variant match is correct. However, the actual overall score (총점) shown for that row is 6.60, not the claimed 6.61. Category breakdown (추론 6.78, 수학 6.07, 글쓰기 5.42, 코딩 7.21, 이해 9.07, 문법 5.07) averages to 6.6033, which rounds to 6.60, corroborating the leaderboard's displayed value. Note the leaderboard also lists two other rows for the same model under different prompt strategies (1-shot: 7.11, cot-1-shot: 6.34) — the claim correctly picked the "default" strategy row, avoiding a variant mixup, but transcribed the score with a 0.01 error (6.61 vs actual 6.60). This is a minor discrepancy possibly due to a rounding/transcription slip rather than a wrong-model or wrong-variant issue. File checked: corpus/models/alibaba/qwen2-57b-a14b.json.]

verificationNotes: Fetched qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen2/ directly: confirms release 2024-06-07, 57.41B total/14B active MoE, 64K context, Apache 2.0, ~30 languages (27 additional beyond English/Chinese, commonly rounded to 30 across Qwen materials). 8 of the original 9 result rows are confirmed verbatim on the cited blog (MMLU 76.5, MMLU-Pro 43, GPQA 34.3, HumanEval 53, MBPP 71.9, GSM8K 80.7, MATH 43, BBH 67). DROPPED the HellaSwag=85.2 row: I confirmed 85.2 is a real number for this model but it appears only in the Qwen2 arxiv technical report (2407.10671), not on the cited sourceUrl (qwenlm.github.io/blog/qwen2/) — that page's benchmark table for Qwen2-57B-A14B has no HellaSwag row at all, so per the verification rule the row must be dropped rather than kept against a source that doesn't show it.
